# Senior Full-Stack Engineer — Orion Software

**Location:** San Francisco, CA (Hybrid — 3 days in office)
**Department:** Product Engineering
**Reports to:** Engineering Manager, Platform Team
**Compensation:** $180,000 – $240,000 base + equity + benefits

## About Orion Software

Orion Software builds the next generation of developer collaboration tools. Our flagship product helps engineering teams ship faster through real-time code review, automated workflows, and intelligent project analytics. We serve 500+ enterprise customers including 30 Fortune 500 companies, and process over 10 million code review events daily.

We are a Series C startup with 200 employees and growing. Our engineering culture values technical depth, clear communication, and shipping iteratively.

## The Role

We're looking for a Senior Full-Stack Engineer to join our Platform team. You'll own the architecture and delivery of core product features, working across the React frontend and Node.js backend. This is a high-impact role where you'll shape technical strategy, mentor engineers, and directly influence the experience of hundreds of thousands of developers.

## What You'll Do

- Design, build, and maintain full-stack features across our React/TypeScript frontend and Node.js/PostgreSQL backend
- Lead technical design for complex cross-team projects, writing RFCs and driving architectural decisions
- Improve application performance, reliability, and scalability as we grow from 50K to 500K daily active users
- Build and optimize real-time collaboration features using WebSocket and conflict-free data structures (CRDTs)
- Own end-to-end delivery: from requirement gathering through production monitoring and on-call support
- Mentor junior and mid-level engineers through code reviews, pair programming, and 1:1s
- Contribute to engineering standards, CI/CD improvements, and developer experience tooling
- Collaborate closely with Product, Design, and Infrastructure teams in a fast-paced agile environment

## What We're Looking For

**Required:**

- 5+ years of professional software engineering experience
- Strong proficiency in TypeScript, React, and Node.js
- Experience designing and building RESTful or GraphQL APIs at scale
- Solid understanding of relational databases (PostgreSQL preferred), query optimization, and data modeling
- Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or GCP), containerization (Docker), and CI/CD pipelines
- Track record of leading technical projects and mentoring other engineers
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills
- BS/MS in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience

**Nice to Have:**

- Experience with real-time systems (WebSocket, SSE, CRDTs)
- Familiarity with distributed systems concepts (event sourcing, message queues, Kafka)
- Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi)
- Contributions to open-source projects
- Experience building developer tools or platform products
- Familiarity with observability tools (Datadog, OpenTelemetry, Sentry)

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend:** React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, Storybook
- **Backend:** Node.js, Fastify, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
- **Infrastructure:** AWS (ECS, Lambda, S3), Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform
- **Observability:** Datadog, Sentry, OpenTelemetry
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions, Playwright, Vitest

## Interview Process

1. Recruiter screen (30 min)
2. Technical phone screen — system design discussion (60 min)
3. On-site (4 hours):
   - Coding: build a small full-stack feature (90 min)
   - System design: design a real-time notification system (60 min)
   - Behavioral: collaboration, leadership, conflict resolution (45 min)
   - Meet the team (30 min)
4. Offer

## Benefits

- Competitive salary + equity (ISOs)
- Health, dental, and vision insurance (100% premium covered for employee)
- Unlimited PTO with 15-day minimum
- $3,000/year learning and development budget
- Home office stipend
- 16 weeks paid parental leave
- 401(k) with 4% match
- Commuter benefits
- Weekly catered lunch and stocked kitchen
